papers.title_en: Making Locality-aware GEMM Compatible with Page-Granularity Placement on Chiplet GPUs
papers.authors: Euijun Chung, Jae Hyung Ju, Hyesoon Kim
papers.evidence: 多芯片GPU上面向近内存计算的局部感知数据放置
papers.tldr: 多芯片GPU中，GEMM的局部感知数据放置与固定页粒度交织不兼容，因为最优映射粒度因工作负载而异。本文提出Chiplet-Contiguous Layout，将芯片本地数据连续存储，使得局部感知放置兼容页粒度，无需修改OS或硬件。在Qwen 3 30B和Llama 3.1 70B的LLM推理与训练GEMM上，该方法分别减少远程HBM流量13.0倍和20.7倍，显著提升性能与能效。
papers.abstract_en: Multi-chiplet GPUs scale compute throughput and high-bandwidth memory (HBM) capacity, but their non-uniform memory system makes locality between chiplets and their data critical to the GPU's performance and energy efficiency. Locality-aware scheduling and data placement identify which data should reside near each chiplet. However, in general matrix multiplication (GEMM), locality-aware data placement often becomes incompatible with a fixed page-granularity data interleaving, since the optimal granularity for mapping data across chiplets varies widely across workloads. We propose Chiplet-Contiguous Layout, a global memory layout that stores chiplet-local data contiguously. Chiplet-Contiguous Layout enables locality-aware placement compatible with page-granularity placement across diverse large language model (LLM) GEMM shapes, without changes to the operating system or hardware. On representative LLM inference and training GEMMs from Qwen 3 30B and Llama 3.1 70B, Chiplet-Contiguous Layout on average reduces remote HBM traffic by 13.0x on Qwen and 20.7x on Llama over 4\,KB interleaving, and by 3.3x and 3.7x over coarse locality-aware placement.

papers.title_en: Interface-engineered oxidation-resistant wafer-level Tantalum-Tantalum thermocompression bonding for 3D integration of superconducting interconnects
papers.authors: Harsh Mishra, Ullas Pandey, Sathish Bonam, Adirae Dinesh, Sai Rama Krishna Malladi, Shiv Govind Singh
papers.evidence: 晶圆级钽-钽热压键合用于三维集成
papers.tldr: 钽表面易形成原生氧化物，阻碍低温低压直接键合，限制超导互连的3D集成。本研究采用超薄金钝化层抑制键合界面氧化，在300°C、4.93 bar条件下实现全晶圆Ta-Ta热压键合，键合强度达169 MPa，同时抑制α-Ta生成。该低热预算工艺为可扩展超导互连三维集成提供了有效策略。
papers.abstract_en: Wafer-level 3D integration of superconducting interconnects requires low-thermal budget bonding processes compatible with superconducting materials. Rapid native oxide formation on tantalum (Ta) surfaces limits low-temperature, low-pressure direct Ta-Ta thermocompression bonding. In this study, we develop an oxidation-resistant bonding process using an ultrathin Au passivation layer to suppress oxide formation during bonding. The engineered interface enables blanket Ta-Ta wafer bonding at 300 $^\circ$C under 4.93 bar, significantly reducing the bonding thermal budget and generation of $α$-Ta across the interface, which potentially improves coherence time as reported in literature. Structural and interfacial analyses confirm oxide suppression and continuous metallic bonding, having a bond strength of 169 MPa. This work demonstrates a low-temperature, low-pressure Ta-Ta thermocompression bonding strategy for scalable 3D superconducting interconnect integration.
